title: Mirabella estevesii
taxonomy: Mirabella estevesii is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. The genus Mirabella is part of the subfamily Ixoroideae within the family Rubiaceae. Further classification of Mirabella estevesii is still a subject of ongoing research. The plant is native to Brazil and its classification is still evolving. It is often studied alongside other species in the Rubiaceae family to better understand its place in the botanical classification system.

intro: Mirabilis estevesii, also known as the "Mirabilis vine," is a type of flowering vine native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Mirabilis estevesii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Mirabilis estevesii include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
